title: BUILDSPACE: digital twins for energy-efficient, climate-resilient cities
user: BUILDSPACE project consortium (coordinator SingularLogic; city validators Piraeus, Riga, Ljubljana)
summary: The EUSPA-funded BUILDSPACE project couples building IoT/BIM data, thermal drone imaging and Copernicus data to build digital twins for energy-efficient buildings and climate-resilient cities.
challenge: Cities need to manage buildings and urban areas more sustainably — prioritising energy renovation and anticipating urban heat and flooding — but lack integrated tools combining building-scale and city-scale data.
approach: BUILDSPACE (Horizon Europe, EUSPA, grant 101082575) couples terrestrial data from buildings (IoT platforms, BIM) with thermal-camera drone imaging and location-annotated satellite data from EGNSS and Copernicus. It generates high-fidelity multi-modal digital twins at building scale and decision-support services at city scale for energy-demand prediction, urban-heat and urban-flood analysis. The Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service helps monitor residential emissions so planners can target the worst-performing buildings, while Copernicus Urban Atlas land-use data underpins city-scale flood-damage analysis.
results: Digital-twin services were validated by the city services of Piraeus, Riga and Ljubljana. The urban-flood application demonstrated in Ljubljana lets users compare flood scenarios and compute land-use-based damage costs across the building stock, using continental-coverage Copernicus Urban Atlas data — making the tool replicable Europe-wide. BUILDSPACE has been showcased to the European Commission as a frontrunner in Copernicus for energy efficiency.
